intent.problem: the real fit() lands at ~6% on the 300-node map, illegible — it only ever zooms out, never to a readable floor
intent.approach: clamp to a readable minimum zoom and add fit-to-selection; never below ~40%
intent.alternative: auto-collapse distant nodes instead of clamping zoom — deferred to the LOD change in render
intent.tradeoff: a very large level may overflow the viewport rather than shrink past legibility
intent.outOfScopeReason: camera__zoomToFit really calls state__levelFitBounds (bodies.json calls[], derived-fn-edges.json) but the map has no camera->state function-level edge, and the bundler contract (tools/novakai/verify/bundle.mjs) only lets a fragment cross-reference GLOBAL ids (root.mmd container nodes); state__levelFitBounds is a PRIVATE id namespaced to the state fragment, so authoring `zoomToFit --> state__levelFitBounds` in camera.novakai.mmd renames it to a dangling `camera__state__levelFitBounds` — proven: novakai:bundle produced that id and novakai:validate then FAILed with 'edge endpoint camera__state__levelFitBounds is not defined'. The module-level dependency is already documented (root.mmd: `camera -.->|levelFitBounds| state`); adding a new global id for one function would be a bigger architectural change than this low-risk clamp change warrants.

intent.problem: nav/search centres a node but never zooms — you still cannot read it
intent.approach: thin camera applier: call the pure state.frameTransform to compute centre+readable-zoom, then write cam + applyCam. NOTE: camera.zoomToNode already does centre-only and is wired into navigator.ts:124 — reconcile evolve-vs-add at review
intent.tradeoff: a void DOM method cannot carry a Keystone-2 behavioural contract; the testable logic lives in frame-transform, this change is verified by the signature gate only

intent.problem: frame-node's value is behavioural (centre AND zoom to a readable, clamped floor), but a void DOM camera method cannot carry a Keystone-2 acceptance contract — E2 only tests pure exported functions
intent.approach: extract the testable core as a pure function in state.ts: node + viewport + desired zoom -> {x,y,z}, clamped to [zMin,zMax]; mirrors how camera already consumes nodeCenter/levelFitBounds
intent.rationale: to be behaviourally contractable the logic must be pure; this is the first plan change to carry a real acceptance contract (the Gap-1 load-test)

fm.name: frameTransform
fm.description: pure camera transform: centre a node in the viewport at a readable, clamped zoom

interfaces.name: frameTransform
returns: { x: number; y: number; z: number }

cases.name: centres the node at the requested readable zoom
clamps a too-large requested zoom to zMax
clamps a too-small requested zoom to zMin

intent.problem: navigator click centres but does not zoom; linear scan only
intent.approach: call camera.frameNode on click; add fuzzy match to the filter

intent.problem: render paints full detail at every zoom — the root cause of the wall of slivers
intent.approach: add a level-of-detail pass: below a zoom threshold collapse modules to labelled blocks

intent.problem: on a 38-module / 300-symbol map, pan-and-hunt is the only way to reach a node
intent.approach: new module: Cmd+K fuzzy jump to any node/symbol/command; selecting frames it

intent.problem: the palette must move the viewport when you pick a result
intent.approach: new dotted edge: palette calls camera.frameNode to fly to the chosen node

intent.problem: bodies.json is a one-shot import (ctx.bodies) — the map drifts from code immediately
intent.approach: new service: watch the repo / LSP hook so node<->source stays live; click opens file:line

intent.problem: no signal when a node's linked source changed since the map was built
intent.approach: new module: badge stale nodes — turns the architecture map into a living doc

intent.problem: inspector source pane shows a static body from bodies.json with no freshness signal
intent.approach: show drift badge + 'open at line'; consume driftDetector output

intent.problem: the inspector needs to know which linked body is stale
intent.approach: new dotted edge: inspector reads driftDetector's per-node freshness

intent.problem: frontmatter carries name/desc/state/interfaces — WHAT exists, never WHY a change is proposed
intent.approach: extend the schema with an intent block {problem,rationale,alternative,tradeoff,risk} — the intent LAYER, on existing nodes
intent.rationale: intent belongs next to the interface it justifies, so review and signature stay co-located

intent.problem: the model has no notion of a proposed change or a phase
intent.approach: add ChangeStatus (add|modify|remove) and Phase to core types — the status + phasing LAYER

intent.problem: ctx.state is the single source of truth but only holds the current diagram
intent.approach: carry a plan overlay sibling: per-node/edge status/phase + verdicts, alongside the base graph

intent.problem: an agent authors plans as chat prose, not as a Novakai artifact — the core gap
intent.approach: new module: an agent-writable plan format the app renders as a reviewable diff

intent.problem: an accepted plan dead-ends; nothing carries it into the build
intent.approach: new module: accepted plan -> tools/buildspec spec (reuses spec-to-stubs + gate)
